title: Samsung Odyssey OLED G5 27" QHD 180Hz OLED Gaming Monitor $397 Delivered @ Amazon AU / Bing Lee
body:
Deal: Samsung Odyssey OLED G5 27" QHD 180Hz OLED Gaming Monitor $397 Delivered @ Amazon AU / Bing Lee

[The Value] Another option.
Alienware AW2726DM 27" 1440p 240Hz QD-OLED Gaming Monitor $390.41 Delivered @ Dell
https://www.ozbargain.com.au/node/964238
No opinion.
[JacobAU] $377 for an OLED is pretty good. Good to see OLEDs coming down in price unlike everything else. If I could put up with 27” I’d pick this up in a heartbeat
[ValenHuang] Did some research on this panel: all-panel brightness 226 nit, with peak 357 nit. So technically it is very close to a HDR400 OLED.
However you have to choose from running 2k 120hz with 10-bit or 2k 180hz with 8-bit.
[Yer] Wow they cheeped out. Running DP 1.2 and HDMI 2.0. What year we in…
[Ranike] Latest firmware actually unlocks the dp to 1.4 so you can get 2k 180hz 10-bit
[djuice] Typical max brightness of around 200 nits~ in SDR mode, is way below most modern VA/IPS LED monitors which have around 350-400 nits. It's not going to be very bright, and might have issues using it during daytime in a semi-lit room.
[tp0] 200 nits is more than enough for a PC monitor in any indoor environment.
[Yer] I suggest getting the AW2726DM mentioned above . The G5 has a matte coating that multiplies the raised blacks in these generations of QD OLED.
[RachC] Is this one recommend for good budget 5070 Iracing player?
[Wonderfool] I was waiting for the mini led monitors, but with OLED prices this low, might not need it.
[FlipperMeTimbers] i got this for ~$350 after an amex samsung deal, i like the matte anti reflection coating as my desk is near a window and it makes the low brightness really negligible. often times i dont even run it at full brightness. wish it had usbc hub
[motion2082] I feel like OLED is one of those technologies where margins are huge for companies like MSI and Asus selling the premium OLEDs. Especially when you see a lot more brands selling OLED for under $1000K
[TechNo1geek] $2 cheaper than Kogan Omega OLED (with kogan First), which one should i get?
[jordnz] good question. Kogan looks like it's using a Samsung panel but has 280Hz and 250 nits brightness so.. looks better on paper than this?
[pinkybrain] looks better on paper than this?
it's a Kogan !!
